deep_dive.title: MSTF-Net: A UAV-Oriented Multi-Spectral Video Segmentation Method via Modality-Robust, Scale-Adaptive, and Consistent Fusion
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-spectral video segmentation is essential for robust scene understanding in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) applications such as city planning, land use monitoring, traffic monitoring, and crowd estimation. While the fusion of RGB and thermal modalities offers complementary information for perception under varying lighting and visibility conditions, two fundamental challenges remain: (1) the modal fusion dilemma, arising from significant discrepancies between RGB and thermal features that obscure complementary cues, and (2) temporal variation, induced by rapid motion and viewpoint changes on UAV platforms, which leads to appearance inconsistency and misalignment across frames. To address these issues, this study proposed MSTF-Net, a modality-robust scale-adaptive fusion framework for multi-spectral video segmentation that effectively models cross-modal fusion and temporal consistency. The Modality Spatial Complementary Suppression and Enhancement (MSCSE) module generates unified instance queries via cross-modal attention and suppresses modality-specific noise using residual-guided discrepancy filtering and consistency constraints. To model temporal dynamics, the Multi-scale Temporal Cross-modality Semantic Consistency (MTCSC) module adaptively adjusts the temporal receptive field based on frame distance, capturing both coarse global context and fine local structure across time. Extensive ablation experiments on public RGB-T datasets demonstrate that MSTF-Net achieves state-of-the-art segmentation performance, especially under challenging conditions such as small targets, occlusion, and modality degradation. Specifically, reached 56.42\% mIoU on the MVSeg dataset and 51.80\% mIoU on the CART dataset, respectively.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-spectral RGB and thermal fusion for UAV remote sensing segmentation

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MSTF-Net proposes a modality-robust fusion method for UAV multi-spectral video segmentation, addressing modal discrepancies and temporal variation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对UAV多光谱视频分割中的模态融合困境和时间变化问题，提出MSTF-Net，通过鲁棒融合机制和尺度自适应策略有效整合RGB与热红外特征，在多个数据集上显著提升分割精度，推动了无人机遥感多模态分割的发展。

quick_skim.title: More with Less: a Large Scale Remote Sensing VLM with a Simple Recipe
quick_skim.abstract: Remote sensing vision-language models are increasingly expected to support open-ended reasoning over Earth Observation data and a variety of tasks. Most recent progress in this area has been driven by remote-sensing-specific architectural designs, often introducing new encoders, alignment modules, or task-specific fusion mechanisms. In this work, we challenge the necessity of such architectural specialization. We show that a generally capable vision-language model can achieve competitive or state-of-the-art performance at challenging remote sensing benchmarks, provided that it is trained at sufficient scale across diverse data and tasks. Our model uses a single language policy that can either answer directly in text or invoke a localization tool for segmentation and grounding. To train this heterogeneous behaviour, we employ a multi-task reinforcement learning framework with adaptive task rewards covering multiple-choice VQA, free-form VQA, captioning, detection, and segmentation across a large variety of input types. Our approach achieves competitive results across a broad set of benchmarks, including high-resolution, multi-temporal, multi-modal and multi-view tasks. Further, as training data scales, our experiments show consistent improvements across most tasks both in and out of distribution, which correlate with per-task data diversity. These findings suggest that, for remote sensing VLMs, data scale is more important than architectural novelty.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: large-scale remote sensing vision-language model for multimodal fusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 大规模遥感视觉-语言模型用于多模态融合
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 大规模遥感视觉-语言模型用于多模态融合
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 大规模遥感视觉-语言模型用于多模态融合
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A large-scale remote sensing VLM achieves SOTA by scaling data and tasks without specialized architectures, providing a simple recipe for multimodal fusion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种简单的遥感视觉-语言模型（VLM）训练方法，通过大规模多样化的数据和任务扩展，无需专门设计的多模态融合架构即可在多个遥感基准上取得竞争性甚至最优性能。该工作挑战了现有专门化设计的必要性，为多模态遥感数据融合提供了通用且可扩展的方案。

quick_skim.title: On the modality gap and the contrastive loss in multi-modal representation learning
quick_skim.abstract: We study the modality gap in CLIP-style dual-encoder contrastive learning, where image and text embeddings remain misaligned despite being trained in a shared space. We argue that the gap is induced by a failure of the InfoNCE formulation with independent encoders. We conduct a uni-modal experiment with two independent encoders and identical initialization conditions and find that InfoNCE actively generates a gap at low temperatures. We provide a theoretical analysis of this phenomenon and show that the modality gap is indeed a mode-failure of InfoNCE, but only at low temperatures. We propose a simple modification called xNCE, which uses intermodal as well as intra-modality negative contrastive pairs. xNCE matches retrieval performance on MS-COCO while consistently reducing the gap even at low temperatures. Notably, xNCE improves zero-shot classification over the InfoNCE baseline across all benchmarks, whereas high-temperature InfoNCE and regularized InfoNCE both fail to do so, demonstrating that xNCE reduces the modality gap without sacrificing the discriminative geometry needed for transfer.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal representation learning and modality gap analysis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态表示学习与模态差距分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态表示学习与模态差距分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态表示学习与模态差距分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper analyzes the modality gap in CLIP-style contrastive learning and proposes xNCE to improve alignment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文探究了CLIP式对比学习中图像与文本嵌入之间的模态差距成因，发现InfoNCE损失在低温下主动制造差距。提出xNCE改进，引入模态内负样本对，缩小了模态差距并提升跨模态检索性能，为多模态学习提供了关键理论见解和实用改进。

quick_skim.title: TabPFN beyond Tabular Data: Calibration and Accuracy on Multimodal Embeddings
quick_skim.abstract: Few-shot multimodal classification commonly attaches a lightweight head, such as $k$-nearest neighbors, logistic regression, or a linear SVM, to a frozen pretrained encoder. Although computationally efficient, these heads can produce poorly calibrated confidence scores, limiting their reliability in calibration-sensitive applications. We evaluate TabPFN as a plug-and-play, zero-gradient classification head for frozen image, text, and audio encoders. Across 22{,}820 evaluation episodes spanning 14 datasets, 11 encoders, and three modalities, TabPFN achieves the best mean rank among nine classification heads on both negative log-likelihood (NLL) and expected calibration error (ECE). At a representative setting, it reduces NLL by 48--62\% and ECE by 2.1--5.3$\times$ relative to the average of the eight baselines while matching or exceeding their average accuracy. Its accuracy advantage is conditional, concentrating at moderate-to-high shot counts and low-to-moderate feature dimensions ($k \ge 50$, $d \le 32$), and diminishing when labeled data are scarce, feature dimensions are high, or competing methods approach ceiling accuracy. In targeted backbone-adaptation experiments, replacing the trained linear head with TabPFN substantially improves calibration while preserving competitive accuracy. These results provide empirical guidance for using TabPFN as a training-free head in calibration-sensitive multimodal classification. To support transparency and reproducibility, we publicly release the source code, experiment configurations, and evaluation scripts in our GitHub repository: https://github.com/Jingxiang-Zhang/tabpfn-multimodal-embeddings.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal embedding classification head; applicable to remote sensing multimodal classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态嵌入分类头，可应用于遥感多模态分类
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态嵌入分类头，可应用于遥感多模态分类
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态嵌入分类头，可应用于遥感多模态分类
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TabPFN evaluated as a plug-and-play classifier head for frozen multimodal encoders, achieving best calibration and accuracy across 14 datasets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文评估了TabPFN作为现成分类头在冻结的多模态编码器（图像、文本、音频）上的性能，实验覆盖14个数据集和11种编码器。结果表明TabPFN在负对数似然和期望校准误差上取得最佳平均排名，有效提升了少样本多模态分类的校准度和准确率。尽管未直接处理遥感数据，但该方法可通用地作为遥感多模态图像分类的分类器头，为需求中的多模态遥感分类提供有效的工具支持。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文评估了TabPFN作为现成分类头在冻结的多模态编码器（图像、文本、音频）上的性能，实验覆盖14个数据集和11种编码器。结果表明TabPFN在负对数似然和期望校准误差上取得最佳平均排名，有效提升了少样本多模态分类的校准度和准确率。尽管未直接处理遥感数据，但该方法可通用地作为遥感多模态图像分类的分类器头，为需求中的多模态遥感分类提供有效的工具支持。
